getting-started--docs.name: Docs
getting-started--docs.title: Getting Started
getting-started--docs.content: import { Meta } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks"; # Getting Started ## Installation ```shell npm i @muenchen/muc-patternlab-vue ``` ## Setup This setup assumes your client app is created with Vite and vue-ts template. In your `package.json`, you shall have the dependencies compatible with the following: ```json "dependencies": { "vue": "^3.5.0" } ``` In your `vite.config.ts`, you shall configure to dedupe `vue`: ```ts export default defineConfig({ resolve: { dedupe: ["vue"], }, }); ``` In your Webcomponents root you should import the MDE5-CSS from assets.muenchen.de and SVG-Sprite for MDE5-Icons, as well as the optional custom-icons SVG-Sprite: ```html ``` Import components from this library in your own component: e.G. Using the MucBanner-Component: ```html ```

iconography--docs.name: Docs
iconography--docs.title: Iconography
iconography--docs.content:
import { IconGallery, IconItem, Meta } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";

<Meta title="Iconography" />

# Iconography

This library already contains MDE5-Patternlabs iconset. The documentation to it can be found [here](https://patternlab.muenchen.space/?p=viewall-guidelines-icons).

Additionally, it adds some specialty icons from [Pictogrammers](https://pictogrammers.com/library/mdi) which can be accessed by embedding the SVG-Sprite `custom-icons.svg` as described in [the getting started guide](/docs/getting-started--docs#setup).

<IconGallery>
  <IconItem name="pencil">
    <svg fill="currentColor">
      <use href="#icon-pencil" />
    </svg>
  </IconItem>

    </svg>
  </IconItem>

  </IconItem>

    </svg>
  </IconItem>

  </IconItem>

    </svg>
  </IconItem>
  <IconItem name="checklist">

  <IconItem name="order-bool-ascending">
    <svg fill="currentColor">
      <use href="#icon-order-bool-ascending" />
    </svg>
  </IconItem>
  <IconItem name="drag-vertical">

  </IconItem>
  <IconItem name="arrow-up-down">

    </svg>
  </IconItem>
  <IconItem name="circle-outline">

</IconGallery>

typography--docs.name: Docs
typography--docs.title: Typography
typography--docs.content:
import { Meta } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks"; # Typography Reference: [https://patternlab.muenchen.space/?p=viewall-guidelines-typography](https://patternlab.muenchen.space/?p=viewall-guidelines-typography) ## Headings For all Headings just use the semantic h1 to h5 HTML Elements - they will automatically be styled according to the MDE Design System. ## Body For the body there are three different sizes. Each with it's own class to be used. For all sizes the additional class 
mde-bold
can be used to make the text bold.
Size CSS Class
Body 1 
mde-b1
Body 2 (smaller) 
mde-b2
Body 3 (smallest) 
mde-b3
